---
title: "sammy_r21 — per-sample bookkeeping"
subtitle: "Cell accounting and per-cell QC across the cohort, at each sample's chosen resolution"
format:
  pptx:
    reference-doc: ouhsc_ppt_template.pptx
    # `#` becomes a Section Header slide, `##` a Title-and-Content slide. Without
    # this pandoc infers the level from the headings present, so adding the first
    # `#` would silently demote every `##` to a bullet.
    slide-level: 2
execute:
  echo: false
  warning: false
  # Keep rendering when a cell raises: a sample whose chosen resolution has no
  # matching leiden column (or whose h5ad predates uns["mito_filter"]) errors on its
  # own slide instead of taking the whole deck down. The cost is that a genuinely
  # broken cell yields an error slide and an exit code of 0 — read the slides, not
  # just the exit status.
  error: true
---

```{python}
# | tags: [setup]
from pathlib import Path

import anndata as ad
import pandas as pd
from IPython.display import display, Markdown

# Every clustered h5ad is staged into this notebook's working directory by the
# Nextflow process, so globbing is the whole input contract — no params to pass or
# drift. These are cluster's h5ads: their cells are past the mito cut, they carry the
# leiden sweep columns, and (when produced by a current cluster.py) uns["mito_filter"].
H5ADS = sorted(Path(".").glob("*.h5ad"))
if not H5ADS:
    raise FileNotFoundError("No .h5ad files staged next to the notebook.")

# The per-sample resolution table is staged flat beside the notebook, same contract as
# the h5ads. n_clusters is reported at the resolution CHOSEN for each sample, not one
# cohort-wide value: the right resolution is a judgement made by reading cluster_report,
# recorded here. A sample missing from this table, or a chosen resolution with no
# matching leiden_<res> column in its h5ad, is an error — bookkeeping silently at the
# wrong resolution is exactly what the per-sample choice exists to prevent.
RESOLUTIONS_CSV = Path("chosen_resolutions.csv")


def load_chosen_resolutions(path):
    """sample -> chosen resolution (float). The CSV carries a leading `#` comment block."""
    df = pd.read_csv(path, comment="#")
    missing = {"sample", "resolution"} - set(df.columns)
    if missing:
        raise ValueError(f"{path}: resolution table needs columns sample,resolution; missing {missing}")
    return dict(zip(df["sample"].astype(str), df["resolution"].astype(float)))


def detected_genes(adata):
    """Number of genes with a nonzero count in at least one retained cell.

    Reads layers["counts"] (raw) when present — cluster.py stashes it before
    normalization; the published h5ad always carries it. Falls back to X, where
    log1p(normalized) is > 0 exactly where the count was, so the detected set is the
    same either way. This is post-filter detection: create_adata's var counts were
    computed before the mito cut and cluster.py does not recompute them, so the matrix
    is the only source that reflects the cells actually kept.
    """
    m = adata.layers["counts"] if "counts" in adata.layers else adata.X
    if hasattr(m, "getnnz"):                       # sparse: cells expressing each gene
        return int((m.getnnz(axis=0) > 0).sum())
    return int((m > 0).any(axis=0).sum())          # dense fallback


def md_table(df):
    """Render a DataFrame as a markdown table.

    Built by hand rather than via df.style (HTML-only, renders as nothing in pptx)
    or df.to_markdown (needs tabulate, which the container does not carry).
    """
    header = "| " + " | ".join(str(c) for c in df.columns) + " |"
    rule = "| " + " | ".join("---" for _ in df.columns) + " |"
    rows = ["| " + " | ".join(str(v) for v in row) + " |" for row in df.itertuples(index=False)]
    display(Markdown("\n".join([header, rule] + rows)))


def summarize(sample, path, resolution):
    """One bookkeeping row for a sample, as a dict.

    Read full (not backed="r"): detected_genes needs the counts matrix, so there is no
    backed-mode saving to be had. This is a fan-in report that runs once, one sample in
    memory at a time, so a full read per sample is affordable.
    """
    adata = ad.read_h5ad(path)

    key = f"leiden_{resolution}"                    # matches cluster.py's key_added
    if key not in adata.obs:
        leiden_cols = sorted(c for c in adata.obs.columns if c.startswith("leiden_"))
        raise ValueError(
            f"{sample}: chosen resolution {resolution} -> '{key}' not in h5ad. "
            f"Available: {leiden_cols or 'none'}"
        )

    # Mito accounting is only in the object if cluster.py wrote uns["mito_filter"]
    # (added when this bookkeeping was). Absent -> the removed/raw counts are
    # unrecoverable from this object; report NA rather than guess.
    mito = adata.uns.get("mito_filter")
    if mito is None:
        n_removed = n_raw = pct_removed = pd.NA
    else:
        n_removed = int(mito["n_dropped"])
        n_raw = int(mito["n_before"])
        pct_removed = round(n_removed / n_raw * 100, 2) if n_raw else pd.NA

    obs = adata.obs
    return {
        "sample": sample,
        "condition": str(obs["condition"].iloc[0]),
        "id_num": int(obs["id"].iloc[0]),
        "resolution": resolution,
        "clusters": int(obs[key].nunique()),
        "cells_raw": n_raw,
        "cells_removed": n_removed,
        "pct_removed": pct_removed,
        "cells_kept": int(adata.n_obs),            # retained (post mito filter)
        "detected_genes": detected_genes(adata),
        # Medians, not means: the per-cell distributions are right-skewed (a debris tail
        # is left in on purpose — see cluster.py), so the median is the more honest
        # "typical cell" here. Swap to .mean() if a downstream comparison wants it.
        "genes_per_cell": round(float(obs["n_genes_by_counts"].median()), 1),
        "counts_per_cell": round(float(obs["total_counts"].median()), 1),
        "pct_mito": round(float(obs["pct_counts_mt"].median()), 2),
    }


resolutions = load_chosen_resolutions(RESOLUTIONS_CSV)

rows = []
for path in H5ADS:
    # sample id is in obs; read it cheaply (backed) before the full summarize read.
    sample = str(ad.read_h5ad(path, backed="r").obs["sample"].iloc[0])
    if sample not in resolutions:
        raise KeyError(
            f"{sample}: no chosen resolution in {RESOLUTIONS_CSV}. Add a row per sample "
            "before bookkeeping — n_clusters is reported at the per-sample resolution, "
            "so there is no safe default."
        )
    rows.append(summarize(sample, path, resolutions[sample]))

# Order the cohort as two blocks, normal then obese, ascending id — the order the rest
# of the pipeline's reports read in.
summary = (
    pd.DataFrame(rows)
    .sort_values(["condition", "id_num"])
    .drop(columns="id_num")
    .reset_index(drop=True)
)

# CSV side output: the full flat table, published alongside the deck. Keeps the chosen
# resolution (which the slide omits) as provenance — n_clusters is reported at it, so
# once samples differ it is the only record of which resolution produced that count.
summary.to_csv("sample_summary.csv", index=False)
```

# Cohort bookkeeping

## Per-sample summary

Per-cell metrics (genes/cell, counts/cell, mito %) are medians.

```{python}
# One consolidated table: cell accounting (raw -> removed -> kept), the cluster count at
# each sample's chosen resolution, and the per-cell QC medians. Compact display headers
# so all of it fits a single slide; the CSV keeps the verbatim column names. Resolution
# is deliberately omitted here (kept in the CSV as provenance). cells_removed / pct_removed
# read NA for any h5ad produced before uns["mito_filter"]. Per-cell metrics (genes/cell,
# counts/cell, mito %) are medians — see summarize().
DISPLAY = {
    "sample": "sample",
    "condition": "condition",
    "clusters": "clusters",
    "cells_raw": "cells raw",
    "cells_removed": "removed",
    "pct_removed": "% removed",
    "cells_kept": "cells kept",
    "detected_genes": "genes detected",
    "genes_per_cell": "genes/cell",
    "counts_per_cell": "counts/cell",
    "pct_mito": "mito %",
}
md_table(summary[list(DISPLAY)].rename(columns=DISPLAY))
```
